metadata.title: AUTOMATIC CLIPPING: DIFFERENTIALLY PRIVATE DEEP LEARNING MADE EASIER AND STRONGER

sections.title: ABSTRACT
content: Per-example gradient clipping is a key algorithmic step that enables practical differential privacy (DP) training for deep learning models. The choice of clipping threshold R is shown to be vital for achieving high accuracy under DP but difficult to tune.
We propose an easy-to-use replacement, called Automatic Clipping, that eliminates the need to tune R for any DP optimizers.

figures.description: Ablation study of clipping threshold and learning rate heatmaps

sections.title: DIFFERENTIAL PRIVACY IN DEEP LEARNING
content: Differential privacy (DP) is a formal definition of privacy that has been shown to prevent the privacy risks in deep learning.
Definition 1. A randomized algorithm M is (ε, δ)-DP if for any neighboring datasets S, S'...

sections.title: PER-SAMPLE GRADIENT CLIPPING
content: Abadi's clipping is the most widely applied clipping function, which requires a hyperparameter - the clipping threshold R.

figures.description: Gradient norms before and after different clipping at R=1

sections.title: CONTRIBUTIONS
content: We propose a new per-sample gradient clipping function that does not require the clipping threshold R.
We give a convergence analysis of automatic DP-SGD in the non-convex setting.
We demonstrate on extensive language and vision tasks that automatic clipping outperforms or matches state-of-the-art.
We demonstrate minimal changes to adopt our automatic clipping in existing codebases.

sections.title: R-INDEPENDENCE OF AUTOMATIC CLIPPING
content: We show that using automatic clipping, it suffices to tune learning rate and weight decay in DP training as in the regular training.
Theorem 1. Non-adaptive R-dependent automatic DP-SGD is equivalent to R-independent automatic DP optimizers.
Theorem 2. Adaptive R-dependent automatic DP optimizers (including AdaGrad, Adam, etc.) are equivalent to R-independent automatic DP optimizers.

sections.title: CONVERGENCE ANALYSIS OF AUTOMATIC DP-SGD
content: We analyze the convergence on non-convex loss with standard assumptions in SGD literature.
Leveraging Theorem 4, our analysis provides guidelines to DP training that match empirical observation.

sections.title: AUTOMATIC CLIPPING IS EASY TO CODE
content: Switching from Abadi's clipping to automatic clipping changes only one line of code.

sections.title: EMPIRICAL RESULTS
content: References: [1] Abadi et al. Deep learning with differential privacy. CCS 2016.

Test accuracy on language tasks with RoBERTa-large parameters
Test performance on E2E dataset with GPT2
